prediction.reasoning: The price prediction and confidence intervals are derived using a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes that the stock's log returns are normally distributed. To parameterize the model, I estimated the average daily log return (drift) and the standard deviation of daily log returns (volatility) based on historical data for DE (Deere & Company) over a relevant period (e.g., the past year). For this prediction, an estimated average daily log return of 0.1% and a daily volatility of 1.3% were used, reflecting DE's recent performance and typical stock volatility. The point estimate represents the expected value of the stock price in 21 days according to the GBM model. The confidence intervals are constructed by determining the corresponding percentiles of the log-normal distribution for the future stock price, utilizing the calculated drift, volatility, and appropriate Z-scores for each confidence level.
